chunks.text: Kannan Ramchandran is a Professor at UC Berkeley's EECS Department. Contact information includes office at 269 Cory Hall, phone 510-642-2353, fax 642-2845, email kannanr@eecs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are Tuesday 3:00-4:00 or by appointment at 258 Cory Hall. Research assistant Stephanie Bratton can be contacted at sbratton@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Kannan Ramchandran's position and contact details?
chunks.answer: Professor at UC Berkeley EECS Department.

chunks.text: He received his B.E. from City College of New York in 1982, M.S. and Ph.D. from Columbia University in 1984 and 1993. After his Ph.D., he became Assistant Professor at University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign and Associate Director of Beckman Institute Multimedia Laboratory. In 1999, he joined UC Berkeley's EECS Department and created the Berkeley Audiovisual Signal Processing and Communication Systems (BASiCS) Lab.
chunks.question: What are Kannan Ramchandran's educational background and career path?
chunks.answer: B.E. from CCNY, M.S./Ph.D. from Columbia University.

chunks.text: Professor Ramchandran is an IEEE Fellow and received the Outstanding Teaching Award from UC Berkeley EECS Department. He won the IEEE Kobayashi award in 2017 for pioneering contributions to distributed source and storage coding theory and practice.
chunks.question: What major awards has Kannan Ramchandran received?
chunks.answer: IEEE Fellow and IEEE Kobayashi award in 2017.

chunks.text: Research areas include Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS), Signal Processing (SP), Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). Research centers involved with are Berkeley BASiCS, Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD), Berkeley Laboratory for Information and System Sciences (BLISS), CLIMB, and Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC).
chunks.question: What are Kannan Ramchandran's research areas and centers?
chunks.answer: IDNCS, SP, AI, CIR and multiple Berkeley centers.

chunks.text: Teaching schedule includes EECS 126: Probability and Random Processes, held Tuesday/Thursday 12:30-13:59 at Dwinelle 145. Selected publications cover topics like blockchain video systems, graph signal processing, correlation clustering, pairwise comparisons, LED array microscopy, and distributed video coding.
chunks.question: What course does Kannan Ramchandran teach and publication topics?
chunks.answer: EECS 126 and diverse signal processing publications.

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include IEEE Koji Kobayashi Computers and Communications Award (2017), IEEE COMSOC & IT Joint Paper Award (2012), Electrical Engineering Outstanding Teaching Award (2009), IEEE Fellow (2004), Okawa Research Grant (2000), and NSF CAREER Award (1997).
chunks.question: What additional honors has Kannan Ramchandran earned?
chunks.answer: Multiple IEEE awards and NSF CAREER grant.
